textPlain: I really liked the README, that was a good use of AI.If you're interested in the idea of writing a database, I recommend you checkout https://github.com/thomasjungblut/go-sstables which includes sstables, a skiplist, a recordio format and other database building blocks like a write-ahead log.Also https://github.com/BurntSushi/fst which has a great Blog post explaining it's compression (and been ported to Go) which is really helpful for autocomplete/typeahead when recommending searches to users or doing spelling correction for search inputs.
Shameless plug, you may wish to do Lucene-style tokenizing using the Unicode standard: https://github.com/clipperhouse/uax29/tree/master/words
Did you vibe code this? A few things here and there are a bit of a giveaway imho.
Would love to hear how this compares to another popular go based full text search engine (with a not too dissimilar name) https://github.com/blevesearch/bleve?

Cool project!I see you are using a positional index rather than doing bi-word matching to support positional queries.Positional indexes can be a lot larger than non-positional. What is the ratio of the size of all documents to the size of the positional inverted index?
Great work! Would be interesting to see how it compares to Lucene performance-wise, e.g. with a benchmark like https://github.com/quickwit-oss/search-benchmark-game

This is very cool! Your readme is intersting and well written - I didn't know I could be so interested in the internals of a full text search engine :)What was the motivation to kick this project off? Learning or are you using it somehow?
looks great! would love to see benchmark with bleve and a lightweight vector implementation.
Why did you create this new account if there's already 3 existing accounts promoting your stuff and only your stuff?
tysm, i love this, FST is vv cool
Got to admit, initial impressions, this is pretty neat, would spend sometime with this. Thanks for the link :)
On my way to make a Dexter meme on thisWhen you think OP vibe-coded the project but can’t prove it yethttps://x.com/FG_Artist/status/1974267168855392371
I put Overview section from the Readme into an AI content detector and it says 92% AI. Some comment blocks inside codebase are rated as 100% AI generated.
Another possible tell (not saying this is vibe coded) is when every function is documented, almost too much comments
What makes you think so?

Bleve is an absolute beast! built with <3 at Couchbase
Fun fact: the folks who maintain it sit right across from me at work

Observation is spot on. Biword matching would definitely ease this. 
Stealing bi-word matching for a future iteration, tysm :D

Thanks! Honestly, given it's hacked together in a weekend not sure it’d measure up to Lucene/Bleve in any serious way.I intended this to be an easy on-ramp for folks who want to get a feel for how FTS engines work under the hood :)

I’m learning the internals of FTS engines while building a vector database from scratch. Needed a solid FTS index, so I built one myself :)It ended up being a clean, reusable component, so I decided to carve it out into a standalone projectThe README is mostly notes from my Notion pages, glad you found it interesting!
tysm, would try pairing it with HNSW and IVF, halfway through :)

Because running a three-account bot‑net farm is fun :D
